note: Minify Source HTML exposes a single config object and one kernel response subscriber; no plugins, no API, no hooks, no admin route of its own. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded) probing documented facts an agent would otherwise read source to answer; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site with the minifyhtml module installed, how do I turn HTML minification on from the command line? Minification is off by default.
evals.expected_output: Set the minify key (default false) on the minifyhtml.config object: drush cset minifyhtml.config minify true.

evals.prompt: Where in the Drupal admin UI are the Minify Source HTML settings, and what config object stores them? The module does not add its own admin page.
evals.expected_output: On the core Performance form (system.performance_settings, Admin > Configuration > Development > Performance) under Bandwidth optimization; settings stored in the minifyhtml.config config object.

evals.prompt: What are the three settings keys in the minifyhtml.config object and their default values?
evals.expected_output: minify (boolean, default false), strip_comments (boolean, default true), exclude_pages (string, default '/admin*').

evals.prompt: Which minifyhtml setting removes HTML comments (and multi-line comments inside script/style tags), and does it also strip IE conditional comments?
evals.expected_output: The strip_comments key (default true) removes HTML comments plus /* */ comments in script/style; IE conditional comments are preserved.

evals.prompt: How do I stop Minify Source HTML from minifying certain paths, and what is excluded by default?
evals.expected_output: The exclude_pages setting takes newline-separated path patterns (leading /, * wildcard, <front> token); it defaults to /admin* so admin pages are skipped.

evals.prompt: How does minifyhtml minify the page, and at what point does it run? Name the class and the event.
evals.expected_output: The MinifyHTMLExit event subscriber runs on KernelEvents::RESPONSE at priority -10000, minifying HtmlResponse and BigPipeResponse content.

evals.prompt: Does minifyhtml add per-request overhead when Drupal's page cache is serving anonymous pages? Explain when minification runs relative to the cache.
evals.expected_output: Minification runs on the RESPONSE event (priority -10000) before the response is stored in the internal page cache, so the cached copy is already minified and cache hits have effectively no minification cost.

evals.prompt: Will minifyhtml collapse the whitespace inside a <pre> or <textarea> block? Which tags does it protect from minification?
evals.expected_output: No. <textarea>, <pre>, <iframe>, <script> and <style> blocks are swapped for placeholder tokens before minifying and restored afterward, so their contents are preserved (ld+json scripts kept byte-safe).
